frameworks.name: latent cause inference
frameworks.one_line: structure learning segments experience into clusters that gate memory updating

frameworks.name: Rescorla-Wagner associative learning
frameworks.one_line: delta-rule CS-US weights are extended to one set per latent cause

frameworks.name: Bayesian structure learning
frameworks.one_line: posterior over latent causes inferred via Bayes' rule under a nonparametric prior

frameworks.name: memory reconsolidation
frameworks.one_line: trace-instability account reframed as inference about which latent cause is active

core_question: When does retrieval modify an old memory trace versus form a new one?
key_assumptions: memory is inferential: traces store statistics tied to latent causes
simplicity (Occam) prior: few latent causes generate sensory data
contiguity prior: temporally proximate events share a latent cause
modification eligibility tracks posterior over the active latent cause
manipulations: presence vs absence of brief CS reexposure before extinction
timing between acquisition, reexposure, and extinction (power-law kernel)
amnestic agents (e.g., protein synthesis inhibitors) post-retrieval
key_findings: model reproduces major Pavlovian post-retrieval modification phenomena
cue reexposure raises posterior that acquisition cause is active, enabling update
reduces to Rescorla-Wagner when only one latent cause is inferred
captures spontaneous recovery, renewal, reinstatement via distinct latent causes
interpretations: memory modification reflects latent-cause inference, not intrinsic trace lability
reconsolidation phenomena unify with extinction recovery under one inferential account
limitations_noted: theory is at cognitive/algorithmic level; biological implementation deferred
spatial proximity in latent-cause inference omitted for simplicity
